title: potentilla multifida var. ornithopoda
intro: Potentilla multifida var. ornithopoda, also known as Silver Bush or Creeping Cinquefoil, is a type of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to Asia and is known for its silver-gray foliage and white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with the leaves and stems being used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The plant is also believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antiseptic, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
